title: Victoria Falls 2-Day Adventure Tour
metaTitle: Victoria Falls 2-Day Adventure Tour | Flights, Hotels & Tours Included
metaDescription: Book a 2-day trip to Victoria Falls, Zimbabwe: round-trip flights, hotel, breakfast, transfers, and guided tours including Victoria Falls Rainforest walk (Zimbabwe side) and White-water rafting on the Zambezi. Prices from $1502 per person (estimate).
highlights: Chobe National Park, Botswana (day trip across the border)
overview: Spend 2 days discovering Victoria Falls, Zimbabwe — Warm, dry-season-dominated climate — highs around 25-30°C most of the year, with a rainy season (November-March) that swells the falls to their most dramatic, spray-heavy volume. May to August for the most spectacular full-volume falls (post-rainy-season), or September-December for better visibility of the rock face and best conditions for activities like the Devil's Pool. This itinerary blends Victoria Falls Rainforest walk (Zimbabwe side), White-water rafting on the Zambezi, Victoria Falls Bridge walk with free time to explore at your own pace, backed by round-trip flights, handpicked accommodation, and a dedicated travel consultant from enquiry through departure.
included: Round-trip economy-class airfare from your selected origin city (upgrade to premium economy or business available)
1 night accommodation in your selected category (Budget, Mid-range, Premium, or Luxury)
Daily local transport as per the itinerary
Guided tours including Victoria Falls Rainforest walk (Zimbabwe side), White-water rafting on the Zambezi, Victoria Falls Bridge walk
All entrance fees for attractions and activities listed in the itinerary
Applicable taxes and OTA service fee
Dedicated travel consultant support from booking through departure
notIncluded: Visa fees and travel document costs (see Visa Information)
Travel insurance (strongly recommended, available as an add-on)
Lunches and dinners except where noted in the itinerary
Optional activities not listed in the day-by-day itinerary
Airline seat selection and baggage upgrades
Any fare difference for premium economy or business class upgrades

itinerary.title: Arrive in Victoria Falls
itinerary.description: Land at Victoria Falls Airport (VFA) and take your included private transfer to your hotel. With limited time on the ground, head straight out to Victoria Falls Rainforest walk (Zimbabwe side) — Trail of viewpoints along the largest curtain of falling water in the world by combined width and height. Round off the evening with a welcome dinner introducing you to local flavors.

itinerary.title: Victoria Falls Highlights & Departure
itinerary.description: Start early with a visit to White-water rafting on the Zambezi — Grade 4-5 rapids below the falls, seasonal availability. Grab a last taste of local life before your private transfer to VFA for your onward flight.

travelTips: Visit both the Zimbabwe and Zambia sides if time allows — each offers different viewpoints and the KAZA UniVisa covers both.
The falls are most voluminous (but spray-obscured) April-June, and clearest for photos September-December.
Book Devil's Pool and bungee jumping in advance, as they are seasonal and slot-limited.
Carry US dollars in small bills — change is often given in a mix of currencies.
With a short stay, book your must-see attractions in advance to avoid losing time to queues.
packingList: Copies of booking confirmations and travel insurance
Lightweight, breathable clothing and a reusable water bottle
A waterproof jacket or poncho for the Rainforest walk — the spray genuinely soaks visitors, hence the name "Mosi-oa-Tunya" (the smoke that thunders)
Water shoes if attempting Devil's Pool
US dollars in small denominations for tips and curio shopping
thingsToDo: Sunset cruise on the Zambezi River
Bungee jumping from Victoria Falls Bridge
Devil's Pool swim at the edge of the falls
Microlight or helicopter "Flight of Angels" over the falls
Rainforest walk to the falls viewpoints
thingsToAvoid: Visiting the Rainforest walk without rain protection — you will get soaked.
Skipping travel insurance that covers adventure activities like bungee jumping and white-water rafting.
Feeding or approaching wildlife, including baboons in town, which can be aggressive around food.
topRestaurants: The Boma Restaurant — African cultural dinner experience ($$$)
The Lookout Café — International, gorge views ($$$)
nightlife: The Boma cultural dinner and drumming show
instagramSpots: Victoria Falls Rainforest viewpoint with rainbow spray
Victoria Falls Bridge over the gorge
hiddenGems: Zambezi National Park game drives (less crowded than Zimbabwe/Zambia falls sides)
Big Tree, an ancient baobab in town
familyActivities: Rainforest walk to the falls viewpoints
adventureActivities: Bungee jumping from Victoria Falls Bridge
Devil's Pool swim at the edge of the falls
Microlight or helicopter "Flight of Angels" over the falls
luxuryExperiences: Suite stay at a gorge-view luxury lodge
Private sunset cruise with a dedicated boat
honeymoonExperiences: Private candlelit dinner overlooking the gorge
budgetExperiences: Free walk across the Victoria Falls Bridge
Rainforest walk on the Zambia side (often cheaper entry)
localEtiquette: Both US dollars and local currencies circulate — USD is widely accepted and often preferred by tourists.
Tipping guides and porters (a few dollars per activity) is customary and appreciated.
Dress is casual, though modest clothing is respectful in town areas away from lodges.
localTransportGuide: Victoria Falls town is small and walkable, including to the falls entrance itself. Most activities and cross-border excursions (rafting, bungee, Chobe day trips) are arranged through your lodge or a local tour operator with transport included.
bestTimeToVisit: May to August for the most spectacular full-volume falls (post-rainy-season), or September-December for better visibility of the rock face and best conditions for activities like the Devil's Pool.
weather: Warm, dry-season-dominated climate — highs around 25-30°C most of the year, with a rainy season (November-March) that swells the falls to their most dramatic, spray-heavy volume.
visaInformation: Most nationalities, including the US, UK, EU, Canada, Australia, and India, can obtain a visa on arrival or the "KAZA UniVisa" (covering both Zimbabwe and Zambia side visits) for around 30-50 USD.
averageFlightTime: ~12.7h from major hubs (estimate; varies by origin — see Suggested Flights)
cancellationPolicy: Cancellations made 30+ days before departure: full refund minus a $99 processing fee. 15-29 days before departure: 50% refund. 0-14 days before departure or no-show: non-refundable. Airline and hotel supplier cancellation terms may be stricter than this policy and apply in addition — your travel consultant confirms exact terms before payment is taken. No online payment is collected on this site; a consultant confirms final pricing and availability with you directly before any booking is confirmed.
termsAndConditions: Pricing shown is per person based on the traveler type and tier selected and is an estimate pending live fare and rate confirmation.
Passports must be valid for at least 6 months beyond the return date for most destinations — confirm the exact requirement for your nationality.
Itinerary order may change due to weather, local conditions, or attraction closures; inclusions remain equivalent.
Solo travelers incur a single-room supplement, reflected in the Solo pricing tier.
This package does not include international travel insurance unless explicitly listed under Included.
Prices shown in a currency other than USD are approximate conversions at the rate noted and are not a payment quote.

faqs.question: Do I need a visa to visit Victoria Falls?
faqs.answer: Most nationalities, including the US, UK, EU, Canada, Australia, and India, can obtain a visa on arrival or the "KAZA UniVisa" (covering both Zimbabwe and Zambia side visits) for around 30-50 USD.

faqs.question: What is the best time to visit Victoria Falls?
faqs.answer: May to August for the most spectacular full-volume falls (post-rainy-season), or September-December for better visibility of the rock face and best conditions for activities like the Devil's Pool. Warm, dry-season-dominated climate — highs around 25-30°C most of the year, with a rainy season (November-March) that swells the falls to their most dramatic, spray-heavy volume.

faqs.question: What's included in this package?
faqs.answer: Round-trip airfare, accommodation, daily breakfast, airport transfers, daily local transport, and guided tours to the attractions listed in the itinerary. See the Included / Not Included sections for full detail.

faqs.question: Can the itinerary be customized?
faqs.answer: Yes — this itinerary is a starting point. Your travel consultant can add, remove, or reorder activities, extend your stay, or upgrade your hotel category before you book.

faqs.question: What is the cancellation policy?
faqs.answer: Cancellations made 30+ days before departure: full refund minus a $99 processing fee. 15-29 days before departure: 50% refund. 0-14 days before departure or no-show: non-refundable. Airline and hotel supplier cancellation terms may be stricter than this policy and apply in addition — your travel consultant confirms exact terms before payment is taken. No online payment is collected on this site; a consultant confirms final pricing and availability with you directly before any booking is confirmed.

faqs.question: Is Victoria Falls safe for tourists?
faqs.answer: Victoria Falls is generally safe for tourists who take standard precautions — avoid poorly lit areas at night, keep valuables secure, and use licensed transport.

faqs.question: What currency should I bring to Victoria Falls?
faqs.answer: The local currency is USD. Carry some cash for smaller vendors — card acceptance can be inconsistent outside major hotels and malls.

faqs.question: Is this package good for a honeymoon or family trip?
faqs.answer: This itinerary suits couples, solo travelers, and small groups equally well — talk to your consultant about tailoring it further.

reviews.author: Liam Larsen
reviews.title: Five stars, will return
reviews.body: Our trip to Victoria Falls was fantastic from start to finish. The hotel was clean and well located, and the guided visit to Victoria Falls Rainforest walk (Zimbabwe side) was a real highlight. Airport transfers were on time both ways.

reviews.author: Mia Santos
reviews.title: So many hidden gems included
reviews.body: Everything was handled for us — flights, hotel, transfers, and tours to Devil's Pool swim (seasonal) and Victoria Falls town center. Great communication from the consultant before departure too.

reviews.author: James Williams
reviews.title: Luxury without the hassle
reviews.body: Our trip to Victoria Falls was fantastic from start to finish. The hotel was clean and well located, and the guided visit to Sunset cruise on the Zambezi River was a real highlight. Airport transfers were on time both ways.

reviews.author: Marco Garcia
reviews.title: Unforgettable trip!
reviews.body: We added a couple of optional activities beyond White-water rafting on the Zambezi and don't regret it. Good value for what was included, and the food recommendations from our guide were spot on.
